title: Ensure use of VPC-native clusters

input_row.title: Ensure use of VPC-native clusters
input_row.description: Create Alias IPs for the node network CIDR range in order to subsequently configure IP-based policies and firewalling for pods. A cluster that uses Alias IPs is called a VPC- native cluster.
input_row.rationale: Using Alias IPs has several benefits: • Pod IPs are reserved within the network ahead of time, which prevents conflict with other compute resources. • The networking layer can perform anti-spoofing checks to ensure that egress traffic is not sent with arbitrary source IPs. • Firewall controls for Pods can be applied separately from their nodes. • Alias IPs allow Pods to directly access hosted services without using a NAT gateway.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. From the list of clusters, click on the desired cluster to open the Details page. Under the 'Networking' section, make sure 'VPC-native traffic routing' is set to 'Enabled'. Using Command Line: To check Alias IP is enabled for an existing cluster, first define 2 variables Cluster Name and Zone and then run the following command: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- format json | jq '.ipAllocationPolicy.useIpAliases' The output of the above command should return Internal Only - General { "useIpAliases": true } if VPC-native (using alias IP) is enabled. If VPC-native (using alias IP) is disabled, the above command will return null ({ }).
input_row.remediation: Alias IPs cannot be enabled on an existing cluster. To create a new cluster using Alias IPs, follow the instructions below. Using Google Cloud Console: If using Standard configuration mode: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. Click CREATE CLUSTER, and select Standard configuration mode. 3. Configure your cluster as desired , then, click Networking under CLUSTER in the navigation pane. 4. In the 'VPC-native' section, leave 'Enable VPC-native (using alias IP)' selected 5. Click CREATE. Using Command Line To enable Alias IP on a new cluster, run the following command: gcloud container clusters create --zone -- enable-ip-alias
input_row.impact: You cannot currently migrate an existing cluster that uses routes for Pod routing to a cluster that uses Alias IPs. Cluster IPs for internal services remain only available from within the cluster. If you want to access a Kubernetes Service from within the VPC, but from outside of the cluster, use an internal load balancer.
input_row.default_value: By default, VPC-native (using alias IP) is enabled when you create a new cluster. The exception is clusters created before GKE version 1.21.0-gke.1500 (September 2021) using the gcloud CLI without --enable-ip-alias."

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field is provided and specifies using the Google Cloud CLI command to check if Alias IPs are enabled on a GKE cluster. This indicates the control can be verified using API calls and command-line tools.
- The rationale explains the benefits of VPC-native clusters which aligns with standard GCP service properties.
- The presence of a command in the audit field that demonstrates how to check the setting suggests the use of Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and confirms automatable checks.
- There is no indication that leading to manual review such as application-level configs or checks that require human judgment.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_vpc_native_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format json` to check the configuration of the GKE cluster.
- The specific property to validate in the output will be `.ipAllocationPolicy.useIpAliases`.
- The expectation for compliance is that the command returns `{ "useIpAliases": true }`, indicating that VPC-native traffic routing is enabled.
- If the property is absent or returns null, it means the VPC-native setting is not enabled, indicating non-compliance. 

Pass/Fail criteria:
- **Pass:** Output contains `{ "useIpAliases": true }`
- **Fail:** Output contains `null` or does not contain the expected property.

By automating this check using the provided command, an organization can efficiently verify compliance with the CIS benchmark control.
